Title: Insights into glandular trichome biology from analysis of organ-specific gene expression programmes in cannabis, hop and tomato

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Implicated in transport of specialised metabolites in cannabis glandular trichomes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome atlas; HOG conservation analysis; GT-specific expression; association with GT-enriched histone marks
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: GT-enriched expression associated with H3K4me3 and H3K56ac
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC115716265 is a conserved GT-expressed ABC transporter candidate across species. 2. It is implicated in specialised metabolite transport in cannabis GTs. 3. Its GT-enriched expression is associated with H3K4me3 and H3K56ac.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate transporter for engineering metabolite export/sequestration in glandular trichomes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as shared GT gene rather than direct functional perturbation target.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions in cutin biosynthesis and trichome morphogenesis, supporting GT structural modification associated with metabolite storage
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative GT GRN analysis; prior literature cited
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GPAT6 is present in the conserved tri-species GT regulatory subnetwork. 2. It is linked to cutin biosynthesis and trichome morphogenesis. 3. The gene likely contributes to conserved GT structural modifications required for specialised metabolism.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for modifying trichome cuticle/storage structures to improve metabolite retention.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated here as common structural/metabolism-support gene, not as the main experimental focus of direct perturbation in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of cannabigerolic acid in the cannabinoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with cannabinoid biosynthesis in cannabis GTs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: GT-specific/shared with hop GT gene cohort
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome atlas; HOG analysis; cited prior functional evidence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC115713185 was identified among cannabis-hop shared GT genes. 2. The paper states LOC115713185 is a functional cannabigerolic acid synthase. 3. Its GT-specific expression supports a role in cannabinoid production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for cannabinoid pathway engineering in glandular trichomes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper relies on previously reported functional characterization rather than new enzyme assay in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Encodes an olivetol synthase copy essential for olivetolic acid assembly in the cannabinoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted to support cannabinoid precursor biosynthesis in cannabis GTs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Species-specific GRN subnetwork; transcriptome atlas; prior literature cited
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC115699293 is one of two PKS4 genes enriched in cannabis GT-specific genes. 2. It is described as encoding an olivetol synthase copy essential for olivetolic acid assembly. 3. It appears as a key target in the cannabis-specific GT subnetwork tied to cannabinoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for boosting cannabinoid precursor flux in trichome metabolic engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted and literature-supported role; not newly biochemically characterized here.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Encodes an olivetol synthase copy essential for olivetolic acid assembly in the cannabinoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted to support cannabinoid precursor biosynthesis in cannabis GTs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Species-specific GRN subnetwork; transcriptome atlas; prior literature cited
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC115700696 is a second cannabis PKS4/olivetol synthase copy in the GT-specific subnetwork. 2. It is linked to olivetolic acid assembly. 3. The gene supports species-specific cannabinoid metabolic specialization of cannabis GTs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for pathway stacking with OAC and prenylation steps.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes polyketide intermediate to form olivetolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represents cannabis GT-specific cannabinoid pathway component
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome atlas; species-specific GT gene enrichment; literature citation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC115723437 is one of two putative olivetolic acid cyclases enriched in cannabis GT-specific genes. 2. The gene fits the expected species-specific cannabinoid biosynthetic specialization. 3. It likely contributes to olivetolic acid formation upstream of cannabinoids.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing cannabinoid precursor synthesis in heterologous systems.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes polyketide intermediate to form olivetolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represents cannabis GT-specific cannabinoid pathway component
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome atlas; species-specific GT gene enrichment; literature citation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC115723438 is a second putative olivetolic acid cyclase enriched in cannabis GTs. 2. It supports the cannabinoid-specific metabolic identity of cannabis GTs. 3. It likely acts in olivetolic acid biosynthesis upstream of major cannabinoids.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with PKS/OAS and prenylation genes for cannabinoid pathway engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a key step critical for bitter acid production in hop glandular trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted core enzymes for bitter acid production in hop-specific GT subnetwork
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hop-specific GRN subnetwork; transcriptome atlas; prior literature cited
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC133816959 and LOC133816960 were identified in the hop-specific GT regulatory subnetwork. 2. They encode phloroisovalerophenone synthases critical for bitter acid production. 3. Their GT-specific expression supports hop-specific specialised metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key pathway genes for enhancing hop bitter acid content.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes O-methylation step in xanthohumol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to hop GT phenolic specialized metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hop-specific GRN subnetwork; previous literature support
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC133802573 is a hop GT target in the species-specific regulatory subnetwork. 2. It encodes desmethylxanthohumol 6'-O-methyltransferase for xanthohumol biosynthesis. 3. The finding links hop GT-specific transcriptional programs to prenylated flavonoid production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for breeding/engineering hops with altered xanthohumol content.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely catalyzes formation of terpene products in tomato glandular trichomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Bound by SlWO and linked to GT terpene metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc05g026600 is a SlWO-bound tomato gene. 2. It encodes a putative terpene synthase consistent with tomato GT defence metabolism. 3. It supports a role for SlWO in regulating GT specialized metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream terpene-pathway target for tuning tomato trichome aroma/defence chemistry.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted influential GT regulator involved in jasmonate-mediated signalling, solute transport, and specialised metabolism-related gene control
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: High-NMS GT regulator validated by DAP-seq; likely coordinates GT function rather than a single metabolite branch
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation; likely activation and repression depending on chromatin context
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Specialised metabolism; jasmonate signalling; solute transport; GT development/function
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: LOC115701324 (AOC); LOC115708820 (CYP94C1-like jasmonoyl-amino acid carboxylase); LOC115697747; LOC115711302
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds promoters of jasmonate biosynthesis/catabolism and transport-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: GT-specific TF; among top NMS-ranked cannabis GT TFs
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: DAP-seq binding sites significantly overlap H3K4me3, H3K56ac, and H3K27me3; suggests context-dependent activation/repression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GRN inference; NMS ranking; DAP-seq; motif enrichment; ChIP-seq histone mark overlap
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC115716879/CsHD-ZIP879 is a top-ranked cannabis GT TF and one of the few experimentally validated by DAP-seq. 2. It binds genes associated with jasmonate signalling, wound response, transport, and specialised metabolism-related chromatin states. 3. Overlap with active and repressive histone marks suggests dual or context-dependent regulatory roles in GT biology.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising master-regulator candidate for engineering cannabis trichome development, signalling, and metabolite productivity.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected by the study for genome-wide target validation; likely one of the central core regulators in cannabis GTs.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted upstream regulator of growth, differentiation, and GT function, potentially acting upstream of CsHD-ZIP879
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: High-NMS validated cannabis GT TF with significant enrichment between DAP-seq targets and GRN predictions
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GT growth/differentiation and broader GT-associated regulatory programs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds developmental and hormone-response genes; predicted to act upstream of CsHD-ZIP879
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Potential feedforward hierarchy through regulation of CsHD-ZIP879
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: GT-specific TF; among top NMS-ranked cannabis GT TFs
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: DAP-seq binding sites significantly overlap H3K56ac and H3K27me3 but not H3K4me3
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GRN inference; NMS ranking; DAP-seq; motif enrichment; overlap with histone marks; hypergeometric enrichment against GRN targets
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC115714116/CsSRS116 is a top cannabis GT-specific TF validated by DAP-seq. 2. Its target genes are associated with growth, development, and cell differentiation. 3. It binds CsHD-ZIP879, suggesting an upstream regulatory role in GT programs.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate upstream regulator for manipulating trichome development and potentially metabolite output in cannabis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the strongest supported GRN regulators because DAP-seq targets significantly overlapped predicted GRN targets.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Terpenes and broader GT specialised metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a master regulator of trichome differentiation and regulates downstream genes linked to RNA biosynthesis, transport, lipid metabolism, cell wall organization, and terpene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Known master regulator of tomato trichome development; validated here by DAP-seq and integrated with GT GRN
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Solyc05g026600; Solyc03g006043; Solyc12g036440; Solyc06g069650; diverse TFs from bHLH, MYB, C2H2, AP2/ERF families
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Direct promoter binding to genes involved in transcriptional cascades, signalling, transport, lipid metabolism, cell wall modification, and terpene synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Tomato GT TF; included in DAP-seq validation set
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: DAP-seq binding sites significantly overlap H3K4me3, consistent with transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GRN inference; prior literature; DAP-seq; motif enrichment; histone mark overlap
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlWO is a central tomato GT regulator with experimentally mapped genome-wide binding sites. 2. Its direct targets span transcription factors, signalling genes, transporters, lipid and cell wall genes, and a terpene synthase. 3. The data support SlWO as a master regulator linking trichome differentiation to specialised metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for improving tomato trichome density, identity, and defensive metabolite production.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes recent evidence that SlWO binds the L1-box and activates GT differentiation pathways.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cannabinoids and other cannabis GT specialised metabolites
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted regulator in cannabis-specific GT subnetwork associated with species-specific specialised metabolism genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Cannabis-specific TF linked to cannabinoid-related GT targets
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CYP81Q family genes; LOC115697587; LOC115699293; LOC115700696
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Species-specific GRN subnetwork inference; literature support for MYB108 in specialised metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOC115723418/MYB108 is one of the cannabis-specific GT TFs. 2. Its predicted targets include cannabinoid precursor-related genes. 3. The TF is proposed to regulate uncharacterised aspects of cannabis GT metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate transcriptional lever for cannabis-specific metabolite engineering.
